response.justification: Bogdan has exceptional technical depth and genuine passion for software craftsmanship and mentoring, but he fails a hard minimum requirement: zero formal people management experience despite the role requiring 2 years minimum. His background is purely technical IC (individual contributor), with teaching and open-source leadership showing mentorship capability but not team management, performance reviews, or organizational leadership. His developer tools experience is scattered and secondary rather than a core specialization. While he could be strong in a senior technical role, he is not qualified for a mid-senior manager position.
strengths: Deep technical expertise across multiple platforms with strong software craftsmanship values (TDD, CI/CD, testing frameworks) and 19 years of development experience
Demonstrated passion for mentoring and knowledge-sharing through workshops, courses, multiple talks, and 10-year open-source project stewardship
Relevant infrastructure experience with CI/CD pipelines, build systems, and developer tooling (GitLab, Docker, Artifactory, testing libraries)
concerns: Zero formal people management experience despite explicit 2-year minimum requirement; no evidence of managing teams, conducting performance reviews, or organizational leadership
No recent technical leadership roles (last potential leadership 2016-2018); currently on career gap for caregiving, raising questions about readiness for people-focused responsibilities

Developer tools experience is secondary and scattered across roles rather than a primary specialization; lacks deep focus on build systems, compilers, or testing infrastructure as main career trajectory
